perturbation.original_text: At the written request of the University or upon expiry or earlier termination of this Agreement, Recipient will on the direction of the University, destroy the Information and will not retain any photocopy or other reproduction of my part of the Information.
perturbation.changed_text: At the written request of the University, Recipient will on the direction of the University, destroy the Information and will not retain any photocopy or other reproduction of my part of the Information. Upon expiry or earlier termination of this Agreement, recipient may retain a single copy of information for record-keeping purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by now allowing the recipient to retain a single copy of information upon expiry or termination of the agreement, while the original text forbids it. This contradicts the original intent to completely eliminate copies of confidential information after the agreement ends, potentially undermining confidentiality and creating uncertainty about the extent of data control. The contradiction arises between the obligation to destroy all copies and the allowance to keep one for record-keeping.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return or Destruction of Information. At the written request of the University, Recipient will on the direction of the University, destroy the Information and will not retain any photocopy or other reproduction of my part of the Information. Upon expiry or earlier termination of this Agreement, recipient may retain a single copy of information for record-keeping purposes.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear contradiction. The original text states the Recipient will not retain any photocopy or other reproduction of the information. The changed text modifies this to allow the recipient to retain a single copy of information for record-keeping purposes upon expiry or earlier termination. This directly contradicts the original statement and the contradicted text, which are identical, creating an in-text contradiction as the recipient is now allowed to keep a copy under certain circumstances while previously, they were not.

perturbation.original_text: No provision of this Agreement will be deemed waived or any breach excused, unless such waiver or consent excusing the breach is in writing and signed by the University. A waiver of a provision of this Agreement will not be constructed to be a wavier of a subsequent breach of the same provision.
perturbation.changed_text: No provision of this Agreement will be deemed waived or any breach excused, unless such waiver or consent excusing the breach is in writing and signed by the University. A waiver of a provision of this Agreement will be construed to be a waiver of a subsequent breach of the same provision.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by stating that a waiver *will* be construed as a waiver of subsequent breaches of the same provision, which directly contradicts the original clause that it *will not* be so construed. This creates an ambiguity regarding the effect of waiving a breach, thus increasing the legal uncertainty and potential for disputes about the enforcement of the contract. The contradiction makes determining the effect of waiving a breach and construing its effect on the whole agreement, a subject for disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Waiver. No provision of this Agreement will be deemed waived or any breach excused, unless such waiver or consent excusing the breach is in writing and signed by the University. A waiver of a provision of this Agreement will be construed to be a waiver of a subsequent breach of the same provision.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text such that 'A waiver of a provision of this Agreement will be construed to be a waiver of a subsequent breach of the same provision.' The contradicted text also states 'A waiver of a provision of this Agreement will be construed to be a waiver of a subsequent breach of the same provision.' This is a direct contradiction of the original text 'A waiver of a provision of this Agreement will not be constructed to be a wavier of a subsequent breach of the same provision.' Thus, the modification in changed text is a direct contradiction of the original and creates an inconsistency in the contract.

perturbation.original_text: The recipient will not assign all or part of this Agreement without the prior written consent of the University.
perturbation.changed_text: The recipient may assign all or part of this Agreement without the prior written consent of the University, provided that the assignee agrees to be bound by the terms of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a direct in-text contradiction by stating that the recipient *may* assign the agreement without consent, while the original text requires the University's prior written consent. This directly conflicts with the original clause establishing control over assignment, thus leading to uncertainty about whether the University's permission is required. The conflict affects the enforceability of the original assignment restriction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Assignment. The recipient may assign all or part of this Agreement without the prior written consent of the University, provided that the assignee agrees to be bound by the terms of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the recipient 'will not assign' without consent, while the changed text says the recipient 'may assign' without consent. This directly contradicts the initial restriction. The contradicted text and changed text are identical, showing the direct contradiction introduced by the modification. This creates a clear inconsistency within the document, because the text now states the recipient can assign without consent when it originally stated they could not without consent, which fulfills the 'Inconsistencies' definition, which states the contradiction should regard deadlines, obligations or penalties. This is a contradiction on obligations, therefore it's a 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement and understanding of the parties with respect to its subject matter and supersedes all prior proposals, negotiations, agreements, understandings, representations and warranties of any form or nature, whether oral or written, and whether express or implied, which may have been entered into between the parties relating to its subject matter.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement and understanding of the parties with respect to its subject matter, except for any oral agreements made prior to the signing of this agreement that are mutually agreed upon by both parties, and supersedes all prior proposals, negotiations, agreements, understandings, representations and warranties of any form or nature, whether oral or written, and whether express or implied, which may have been entered into between the parties relating to its subject matter.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that the agreement contains the entire understanding, *except* for oral agreements made prior. The clause that says the agreement contains the entire understanding, conflicts with any addition or acceptance of oral agreements. It undermines the integrity of the written contract, creating uncertainty about which terms govern the relationship. This has the potential to affect dispute resolution and reliance on the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Entire Agreement and Counterpart. This Agreement contains the entire agreement and understanding of the parties with respect to its subject matter, except for any oral agreements made prior to the signing of this agreement that are mutually agreed upon by both parties, and supersedes all prior proposals, negotiations, agreements, understandings, representations and warranties of any form or nature, whether oral or written, and whether express or implied, which may have been entered into between the parties relating to its subject matter.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear contradiction to the original text. The original text states the agreement contains the entire agreement, while the changed text adds an exception for oral agreements made prior. This creates a direct conflict within the agreement, as the 'Entire Agreement' clause is directly undermined by the exception. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', as it creates a conflict within the agreement. The Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, meaning that the contradiction exists in that clause.
